findings: Dual-write bundle pattern: Commanders write their final bundle to TWO locations: progress_dir/bundle.json (commander-specific) AND BASE_DIR/results/ (centralized). This enables both per-commander discovery and unified result aggregation.
Consistent commander paths: Progress directories follow BASE_DIR/commanders/commander-{NNN}/ with atoms/ and logs/ subdirs created at launch time.
Asymmetric worker vs commander results: Standard workers write only to results/, commanders write to both locations.
Discovery mechanism: Launcher finds completed bundles via find BASE_DIR/results -name *.json -not -name .tmp-* which catches both worker results and commander bundle copies.
Atomic write protocol: Uses .tmp- prefix + rename pattern (tmp.replace(path)), consistent with POSIX atomic rename guarantees.
Documentation gap: README documents {queue,claimed,results,logs} but omits commanders/ subdirectory and dual bundle.json locations.
